name: QA 1998
description:

Testing PCP RDS PMDA - install, remove and values.

>     Data Type: 32-bit unsigned int  InDom: PM_INDOM_NULL 0xffffffff

> Help:
> number of Linux scsi tape devices
>     value 0

> hinv.nfchost PMID: 60.91.16 [Number of fibre channel host bus adapters from /sys/class/fc_host/host*]
>     Data Type: 32-bit unsigned int  InDom: PM_INDOM_NULL 0xffffffff
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> Number of fibre channel host bus adapters from /sys/class/fc_host/host*
>     value 0
> 
> hinv.map.scsi PMID: 60.15.0 [list of active SCSI devices]
>     Data Type: string  InDom: 60.11 0xf00000b
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> There is one string value for each SCSI device active in the system,
> as extracted from /proc/scsi/scsi. The external instance name
> for each device is in the format scsiD:C:I:L where
> D is controller number, C is channel number, I is device ID
> and L is the SCSI LUN number for the device. The values for this
> metric are the actual device names (sd[a-z] are SCSI disks, st[0-9]
> are SCSI tapes and scd[0-9] are SCSI CD-ROMS.
>     inst [0 or "scsi0:0:0:0_Direct-Access"] value "sda"
> 
> hinv.map.scsi_id PMID: 60.0.103 [scsi disk physical device unique identifier]
>     Data Type: string  InDom: 60.1 0xf000001
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> The unique identifier (e.g World Wide Id) of a scsi device. SCSI path
> aliases to the same physical device share the same ID string, e.g.
> for multipath scsi path names. This can be used to aggregate traffic
> statistics to each physical device and to determine the proportion
> of traffic over different paths. See also multipathd(1), where the
> same ID strings (WWID) are used to identify different paths to each
> physical device.
>     inst [0 or "sda"] value "3600224808976f04b90cc3b0a4646aa76"
> 
> hinv.map.cpu_num PMID: 60.18.6 [logical to physical CPU mapping for each CPU]
>     Data Type: 32-bit unsigned int  InDom: 60.0 0xf000000
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> logical to physical CPU mapping for each CPU
>     inst [0 or "cpu0"] value 0
>     inst [1 or "cpu1"] value 1
>     inst [2 or "cpu2"] value 2
>     inst [3 or "cpu3"] value 3
> 
> hinv.map.cpu_node PMID: 60.18.8 [logical CPU to NUMA node mapping for each CPU]
>     Data Type: 32-bit unsigned int  InDom: 60.0 0xf000000
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> logical CPU to NUMA node mapping for each CPU
>     inst [0 or "cpu0"] value 0
>     inst [1 or "cpu1"] value 0
>     inst [2 or "cpu2"] value 0
>     inst [3 or "cpu3"] value 0
> 
> hinv.map.dmname PMID: 60.54.13 [per-device-mapper device persistent name mapping to dm-[0-9]*]
>     Data Type: string  InDom: 60.24 0xf000018
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> per-device-mapper device persistent name mapping to dm-[0-9]*
> No value(s) available!
>

> hinv.map.scsi PMID: 60.15.0 [list of active SCSI devices]
>     Data Type: string  InDom: 60.11 0xf00000b
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> There is one string value for each SCSI device active in the system,
> as extracted from /proc/scsi/scsi. The external instance name
> for each device is in the format scsiD:C:I:L where
> D is controller number, C is channel number, I is device ID
> and L is the SCSI LUN number for the device. The values for this
> metric are the actual device names (sd[a-z] are SCSI disks, st[0-9]
> are SCSI tapes and scd[0-9] are SCSI CD-ROMS.
>     inst [0 or "scsi0:0:0:0_Direct-Access"] value "sda"

> hinv.map.scsi_id PMID: 60.0.103 [scsi disk physical device unique identifier]
>     Data Type: string  InDom: 60.1 0xf000001
>     Semantics: discrete  Units: none
> Help:
> The unique identifier (e.g World Wide Id) of a scsi device. SCSI path
> aliases to the same physical device share the same ID string, e.g.
> for multipath scsi path names. This can be used to aggregate traffic
> statistics to each physical device and to determine the proportion
> of traffic over different paths. See also multipathd(1), where the
> same ID strings (WWID) are used to identify different paths to each
> physical device.
>     inst [0 or "sda"] value "3600224800e744c80f15f8caab6cffc1a"
